sourceMovie.title: Snatch
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Snatch for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Guy Ritchie keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Snatch clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Snatch?
faq.answer: Try Hot Fuzz, The Gentlemen, Shoot 'Em Up—each shares DNA with Snatch while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Snatch?
faq.answer: Snatch (2000) is best described as Crime, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Snatch worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
